Cartier Cloche Collection. The Cartier Cloche watch dates back to [year], making it one of Cartier’s rarest series. Named for its unique case shape—when laid flat, it resembles a "desk bell," as "Cloche" means "bell" in French. This distinctive design allows the case to stand upright on a desk like a miniature clock. Sizes: Large mm, Small mm.

The prototype launched years ago has been included in Cartier’s archives. Its pebble-like rounded case cleverly merges a circular case with a square dial, presenting a minimalist and elegant aesthetic.  
As a milestone in Cartier’s watchmaking history, these masterpieces excel in shaped watches and are highly sought after by enthusiasts, with rarity increasing daily.  
Features include square Roman numeral hour markers, an inner track for precise time reading, blue steel sword-shaped hands, and a beaded crown set with a blue synthetic gemstone.  
Precision is achieved only after countless adjustments.  
The imported calf leather strap is carefully selected, matching the original in color and texture.  
Water-resistant for daily use.

The Cartier Cloche desk clock from the CC gold series is one of the rarest designs in Cartier’s watchmaking history. Introduced in [year], this bell-shaped watch has been issued only a dozen times over a century. This rarity does not reflect its popularity; on the contrary, Cloche is among Cartier’s most iconic pieces. 

Cloche breaks traditional wristwatch conventions—it can be worn or removed and placed on a bedside table as a miniature desk clock, embodying a subtle theatricality that perfectly captures early 20th-century European modern art spirit. The [year] CC version is one of the rarest, with just over a hundred pieces produced. This edition slightly enlarges the original Cloche, featuring sharper, more modern case lines. The [year] Rive version inherits the CC’s design traits and is highly praised among collectors. Available in two sizes: [mm] and [mm].

Cloche breaks traditional wristwatch conventions—it can be worn or removed and placed on a bedside table as a miniature desk clock, embodying a subtle theatricality that perfectly captures early 20th-century European modern art spirit. The [year] CC version is one of the rarest, with just over a hundred pieces produced. This edition slightly enlarges the original Cloche, featuring sharper, more modern case lines. The [year] Rive version inherits the CC’s design traits and is highly praised among collectors. Available in two sizes: [mm] and [mm].

Cartier is the dream itself. The Coussin de Cartier, the most eye-catching design in Cartier’s latest high jewelry watches, features a new cushion-shaped case measuring mm. Traditionally, the cushion shape is a square with rounded corners, but Cartier ingeniously transforms the four corners into swirling lines set with gemstones. This swirl design not only disrupts the expected geometry but also creates a hypnotic effect, evoking a dreamlike, multi-dimensional world reminiscent of "Inception," as if the entire world is turned upside down. The shape is mesmerizing: no longer just square, it blends square and swirling curves.

The Coussin de Cartier watch comes in various sizes with gem-set versions, combining the cushion case with these whirlwind-like lines. This new case shape, inspired by creative geometry and imagination, marks another stunning achievement in Cartier’s design legacy.
